title: Mathematical Literacy
examination_body: Department of Basic Education, Republic of South Africa
level: Further Education and Training (FET) Phase - Grade 10
description: Mathematical Literacy in the FET phase equips learners with the ability to use foundational mathematical concepts and techniques to solve real-life problems. The curriculum emphasizes understanding authentic scenarios and applying both mathematical and non-mathematical reasoning. It develops critical thinking, problem-solving, and communication skills essential for navigating everyday situations and making informed decisions.

topics.title: Rounding Numbers
topics.description: Focuses on understanding and applying appropriate rounding techniques in various numerical contexts.

topics.title: Proportion
topics.description: Explores direct and indirect proportion, enabling learners to analyze relationships and compare quantities in real-life scenarios.

topics.title: Rates
topics.description: Covers the calculation and interpretation of rates, such as comparing prices, to make informed decisions.

topics.title: Percentages
topics.description: Focuses on applying percentages to understand relative sizes and making comparisons in diverse contexts.

topics.title: Representing Relationships (Tables, Graphs, Equations)
topics.description: Develops skills in identifying and representing various relationships using tables, graphs, and equations, including graphical representation of costs.

topics.title: Financial Documents
topics.description: Involves making sense of common financial documents, such as household bills, and extracting relevant information.

topics.title: Income, Expenditure, and Budgets
topics.description: Focuses on understanding, creating, and analyzing income and expenditure statements, including developing household budgets and managing profit/loss.

topics.title: Measuring Length and Distance
topics.description: Emphasizes the accurate measurement of length and distance using appropriate tools and techniques.

topics.title: Perimeter, Area, and Volume
topics.description: Covers the calculation of perimeter, area, and volume, applying these concepts in practical tasks like garden design and costing.

topics.title: Time
topics.description: Involves understanding and performing calculations related to time, often in the context of planning activities.

topics.title: Scale (Maps & Plans)
topics.description: Explores the concept of scale in maps and plans, enabling learners to understand how resizing affects representations of the physical world.

topics.title: Map Interpretation
topics.description: Focuses on interpreting maps to find locations and determine routes, as demonstrated in tasks like "Finding your way."

topics.title: Plan Interpretation
topics.description: Involves understanding and interpreting plans, including tasks like writing instructions for assembling objects.

topics.title: Data Collection and Questionnaires
topics.description: Focuses on understanding and creating questionnaires or surveys to gather information from various contexts.

topics.title: Classifying and Organising Data
topics.description: Covers sorting, counting, and distinguishing between different types of data (categorical, numerical, discrete, continuous).

topics.title: Measuring Data
topics.description: Involves identifying maximum and minimum values within a dataset to understand its range and characteristics.

topics.title: Representing Data
topics.description: Focuses on reading and interpreting information presented in various graphical formats, such as charts and graphs.

topics.title: Expressions of Probability
topics.description: Teaches learners to identify percentage chances and understand key terms associated with probability, such as event and outcome.

topics.title: Probability Prediction
topics.description: Involves making and evaluating predictions based on probability, such as rainfall forecasts or other real-world likelihoods.

topics.title: Evaluating Probability Expressions
topics.description: Focuses on critically assessing probability statements and scenarios to determine their validity and implications.
